meta.name: ASI-3 Anxiety Sensitivity Index
meta.description: The ASI-3 is an 18-item self-report scale measuring anxiety sensitivity, which is the fear of anxiety-related sensations based on beliefs that these sensations have harmful consequences. It assesses three dimensions: physical, cognitive, and social concerns.
meta.author: Taylor et al. (2007)
meta.reference: Taylor S, Zvolensky MJ, Cox BJ, Deacon B, Heimberg RG, Ledley DR, Abramowitz JS, Holaway RM, Sandin B, Stewart SH, Coles M, Eng W, Daly ES, Arrindell WA, Bouvard M, Cardenas SJ. Robust dimensions of anxiety sensitivity: development and initial validation of the Anxiety Sensitivity Index-3. Psychol Assess. 2007;19(2):176-188.
meta.instruction: Please indicate the degree to which you agree with each item by selecting the most appropriate option.

questions.text: It is important for me not to appear nervous
When I cannot keep my mind on a task, I worry that I might be going crazy
It scares me when my heart beats rapidly
When my stomach is upset, I worry that I might be seriously ill
It scares me when I am unable to keep my mind on a task
When I tremble in the presence of others, I fear what people might think of me
When my chest feels tight, I get scared that I will not be able to breathe properly
When I feel pain in my chest, I worry that I am going to have a heart attack
I worry that other people will notice my anxiety
When I feel spacey or spaced out I worry that I may be mentally ill
It scares me when I blush in front of people
When I notice my heart skipping a beat, I worry that there is something seriously wrong with me
When I begin to sweat in a social situation, I fear people will think negatively of me
When my thoughts seem to speed up, I worry that I might be going crazy
When my throat feels tight, I worry that I could choke to death
When I have trouble thinking clearly, I worry that there is something wrong with me
I think it would be horrible for me to faint in public
When my mind goes blank, I worry there is something terribly wrong with me

ranges.label: Low Anxiety Sensitivity
ranges.description: Your overall anxiety sensitivity is low. You are generally not overly concerned about anxiety-related sensations. This indicates that your anxiety levels are within the normal range, and your psychological state is relatively calm.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your balanced perspective on bodily sensations
Your low anxiety sensitivity is a protective factor
Continue maintaining your healthy lifestyle and coping strategies
Practice daily relaxation activities such as deep breathing or stretching
Keep up a regular exercise routine

ranges.label: Moderate Anxiety Sensitivity
ranges.description: You have a moderate level of anxiety sensitivity. Some anxiety-related sensations may cause you concern. At this level, you may find it difficult to relax, experience frequent worry, and notice effects on sleep or concentration.
suggestions: Learn about the nature of anxiety and its physical symptoms
Practice relaxation techniques to manage physical sensations
Challenge catastrophic interpretations of bodily sensations
Consider consulting a counselor or psychiatrist
Cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) is highly effective for anxiety

ranges.label: High Anxiety Sensitivity
ranges.description: Your anxiety sensitivity is high. You may frequently misinterpret normal bodily sensations as dangerous. Your anxiety level is elevated and may be accompanied by physical discomfort and persistent tension. Speaking with a professional is recommended.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional
Interoceptive exposure therapy can be very effective
Cognitive restructuring can help change fearful beliefs about sensations
Schedule a professional assessment as soon as possible
A combination of medication and psychotherapy may be beneficial

ranges.label: Very High Anxiety Sensitivity
ranges.description: Your anxiety sensitivity is very high. This level is strongly associated with panic disorder and other anxiety conditions. Your anxiety level is elevated and may be accompanied by physical discomfort and persistent tension. Speaking with a professional is recommended.
suggestions: Strongly recommended to seek professional help
Evidence-based treatments are highly effective for anxiety sensitivity
Discuss cognitive behavioral therapy options with a professional
Schedule a professional assessment as soon as possible
A combination of medication and psychotherapy may be beneficial

physical.label: Low
physical.description: Low concern about physical anxiety sensations. This indicates that your anxiety levels are within the normal range, and your psychological state is relatively calm.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your healthy lifestyle and coping strategies
Practice daily relaxation activities such as deep breathing or stretching
Keep up a regular exercise routine
Cultivate mindful awareness habits
Maintain strong social connections

physical.label: Moderate
physical.description: Moderate concern about physical anxiety sensations. At this level, you may find it difficult to relax, experience frequent worry, and notice effects on sleep or concentration.
suggestions: Consider consulting a counselor or psychiatrist
Cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) is highly effective for anxiety
Practice relaxation techniques daily and maintain regular exercise
Identify and reduce avoidance behaviors; gradually face anxiety-provoking situations
Maintain regular sleep and exercise habits

physical.label: High
physical.description: High concern about physical anxiety sensations. Your anxiety level is elevated and may be accompanied by physical discomfort and persistent tension. Speaking with a professional is recommended.
suggestions: Schedule a professional assessment as soon as possible
A combination of medication and psychotherapy may be beneficial
Learn to recognize and manage panic attacks
Avoid avoidance behavior; gradually face fears under professional guidance
Ensure you have a reliable support system

physical.label: Very High
physical.description: Very high concern about physical anxiety sensations. Your anxiety level is elevated and may be accompanied by physical discomfort and persistent tension. Speaking with a professional is recommended.
suggestions: Schedule a professional assessment as soon as possible
A combination of medication and psychotherapy may be beneficial
Learn to recognize and manage panic attacks
Avoid avoidance behavior; gradually face fears under professional guidance
Ensure you have a reliable support system

cognitive.label: Low
cognitive.description: Low concern about cognitive anxiety symptoms. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

cognitive.label: Moderate
cognitive.description: Moderate concern about cognitive anxiety symptoms. At this level, psychological concerns may be affecting your daily life. It is recommended to take active steps and consider available support options.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional
Identify your specific sources of concern
Maintain a structured daily routine
Try relaxation and coping methods that suit you
Engage in meaningful activities and social interactions

cognitive.label: High
cognitive.description: High concern about cognitive anxiety symptoms. Your mental health concerns are notable and may be impacting daily functioning. Speaking with a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible
Make sure you have trusted people around you for support
Follow the treatment plan recommended by your professional
Maintain basic daily self-care routines
If you feel overwhelmed, call a mental health helpline for immediate support

cognitive.label: Very High
cognitive.description: Very high concern about cognitive anxiety symptoms. Your mental health concerns are notable and may be impacting daily functioning. Speaking with a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible
Make sure you have trusted people around you for support
Follow the treatment plan recommended by your professional
Maintain basic daily self-care routines
If you feel overwhelmed, call a mental health helpline for immediate support

social.label: Low
social.description: Low concern about social consequences of anxiety. This indicates that your social functioning and relationships are in good condition, maintaining satisfactory social connections.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your current social relationships
Keep an open and communicative attitude
Participate in social activities that interest you
Focus on the quality rather than quantity of relationships

social.label: Moderate
social.description: Moderate concern about social consequences of anxiety. At this level, social difficulties may cause feelings of isolation or discomfort in interpersonal interactions, affecting life satisfaction.
suggestions: Consider discussing social concerns with a counselor
Join social skills training or support groups
Gradually challenge social avoidance behaviors
Develop one or two deeper friendships
Focus on building self-worth

social.label: High
social.description: High concern about social consequences of anxiety. Your social concerns are notable and may be significantly affecting your relationships and quality of life. Professional support is recommended.
suggestions: Seek professional counseling support
Professional treatment for social anxiety or loneliness can be very effective
Start practicing gradually in safe social environments
Ensure basic interpersonal connections and support
Develop a social improvement plan under professional guidance

social.label: Very High
social.description: Very high concern about social consequences of anxiety. Your social concerns are notable and may be significantly affecting your relationships and quality of life. Professional support is recommended.
suggestions: Seek professional counseling support
Professional treatment for social anxiety or loneliness can be very effective
Start practicing gradually in safe social environments
Ensure basic interpersonal connections and support
Develop a social improvement plan under professional guidance
